pi_items.source: Calculated from synthetic data in setup_task.sh: 2015 Forest (50 km²) + Farm (50 km²) = 100 km². Also 2025 Forest (25 km²) + Urban (25 km²) + Farm (50 km²) = 100 km²
Calculated from spatial overlap: 2015 Forest area (0-5000, 0-10000) partially overlaps 2025 Urban (0-5000, 0-5000) = 25 km² transition area
Calculated from spatial overlap: 2015 Forest area (0-5000, 5000-10000) remains Forest in 2025 = 25 km² stable area
Calculated from spatial overlap: 2015 Farm (5000-10000, 0-10000) completely overlaps 2025 Farm (5000-10000, 0-10000) = 50 km² stable area
Confirmed in verifier.py line 49 and used consistently in area comparison logic (lines 103, 116, 128)

privileged_info_summary: The task performs a Union overlay operation on synthetic land use data in UTM Zone 10N (EPSG:32610). The synthetic data represents a 10km × 10km study area (100 km² total). The 2015 layer contains Forest (50 km², west half) and Farm (50 km², east half). The 2025 layer shows transitions: Forest area split into Forest remaining (25 km², northwest quadrant) and Urban expansion (25 km², southwest quadrant), while Farm remains unchanged (50 km², entire east half). The expected Union output will contain 4 resulting features with attributes from both years. Area calculation uses the formula $area / 1000000 to convert from square meters to square kilometers. Expected output is a CSV file at /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/change_matrix.csv with forest→urban transition of 25.0 km², forest→forest stable area of 25.0 km², farm→farm stable area of 50.0 km², for a total of 100.0 km² (with 1.0 km² tolerance for floating-point precision).
